---
title: "Embedding interactive elements in Quarto"
subtitle: Producing and Designing HTML Course Materials, Edinburgh
author: Tom Coleman
date: 2026-08-06
format: 
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, starmast-presentation-blue.scss]
    title-slide-attributes: 
      data-background-image: slides-bg-withlogo.png

---

## Context {data-background-image="slides-bg.png"}

- Lecturer (Education Focused) in mathematics

- passion for creating good resources / very shallow technical background

- currently using Quarto to produce **publicly available free-to-use, accessible and inclusive** maths and stats support resources

- **co-create these resources with students** as part of a Vertically Integrated Project module at St Andrews

## Methods (1/2) {data-background-image="slides-bg.png" .smaller}

wide range of topics necessitate wide range of methods for best results

- R package **webexercises** for 'quick-check' questions (true or false, multiple choice...)

- Quarto's synergy with R encourages **R Shiny apps**, particularly good for statistics

    - works best with a dedicated R Shiny server (money)
    
    - but can use R Shiny Live to run these apps without a server (free, at the cost of longer loading times)

- **Desmos** is best for graphs / recognisable brand for students / accessibility features built-in

    - can use Desmos API environment for full editability (money)
    
    - can embed directly from Desmos without API (free, restricted features)

- raw **html** provides natural flexibility for everything else (calculators, animations...)

## Methods (2/2) {data-background-image="slides-bg.png" .smaller}

**generative AI provides starting points for use of these tools**

- **R Shiny Assistant** (for R Shiny) / **Copilot** (for html) / **ChatGPT** (for html/Desmos API environment)

- teachable moments for students in using generative AI responsibly

- have to ask specifically for accessibility features (standard genAI)

**use conditional content tags if using multiple outputs**

```{.quarto}
::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
interactive content
:::

::: {.content-hidden when-format="html"}
non-interactive content for printable versions
:::
```


## How to embed {data-background-image="slides-bg.png" .smaller}

:::{.panel-tabset}

### html

```{.quarto}
(three ticks){=html}
embeddable content
(three ticks)
```

### Desmos

**with API key**

```{.quarto}
format:
  html:
    include-in-header: 
    - text: <script src="desmos_API_key_link"></script>
```

then use [Desmos' documentation to start building (clickable)](https://www.desmos.com/api/v1.12/docs/index.html) (step 1 covered by above)

**without API key** 

- draw graph using Desmos graphical calculator
- share button / 'embed snapshot'
- paste iframe code into html tags in Quarto

```{.quarto}
(three ticks){=html}
Desmos iframe
(three ticks)
```

### R Shiny

**for Shinylive, no server**

- install shinylive quarto extension [link to github](https://github.com/quarto-ext/shinylive)

- in _quarto.yml file (or put filter on each relevant .qmd file)

```{.quarto}
format:
  html:
    include-in-header: 
    - text: <script src="https://esm.sh/shinylive-r"></script>
    filters:
    - shinylive
```

- in .qmd file

```{.quarto}
[three ticks]{shinylive-r}
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 500

R Shiny app here
[three ticks]
```

### webexercises

- install the `webexercises` package [(link to package with documentation)](https://psyteachr.github.io/webexercises/index.html)

- add files to same folder as the Quarto project as instructed

- load library in each .qmd file

```{.quarto}
[three ticks]{r, setup, include = FALSE}
library("webexercises")
[three ticks]
```

- follow documentation to create `webex-boxes` where you want questions in your text

:::

## Examples (all images links) {data-background-image="slides-bg.png" .smaller}

:::{.panel-tabset}

### Desmos

[![Desmos figure from Guide: Introduction to partial differentiation, written by a fourth-year student](temse-5.png){width=60% fig-alt="Desmos figure from Guide: Introduction to partial differentiation, written by a fourth-year student. It demonstrates a surface with a slider moving back and forth, and the curve beneath responds to the slider."}](../studyguides/introtopartialdifferentiation.qmd)

### R Shiny 1

[![R Shiny figure from Factsheet: Normal distribution, written by a first-year student](temse-6.png){width=50% fig-alt="R Shiny figure from Factsheet: Normal distribution, written by a first-year student. It is a picture of an interactive normal distribution figure, with sliders to modify the mean and the standard deviation."}](../factsheets/f-normaldist.qmd)

### R Shiny 2

[![R Shiny interactive figure from Guide: Introduction to hypothesis testing, written by a fourth-year student](temse-7.png){fig-alt="R Shiny interactive figure from Guide: Introduction to hypothesis testing, written by a fourth-year student. It is a picture of the front page of interactive hypothesis testing flowchart, with buttons to decide on certain directions."}](../studyguides/hypothesistesting.qmd)
  
### html 1

[![html interactive figure from Guide: Matrix multiplication, written by a fourth-year student](html1.png){width=60% fig-alt="html interactive figure from Guide: Matrix multiplication, written by a fourth-year student. It is a picture of the demonstration of the multiplication of a 2x3 and 3x2 matrix, with one cell highlighted."}](../studyguides/matrixmultiplication.qmd)

### html 2

[![html interactive figure from Calculator: HCF and LCM](html2.png){width=50% fig-alt="html interactive figure from Calculator: HCF and LCM. It is a picture of the calculator, with an example of 16 and 25. Buttons ask you to calculate HCF, LCM, or both."}](../apps/calculators/c-hcflcm.qmd)

### webexercises

[![webexercises from Guide: Rationalizing the denominator, written by a second-year student](webex.png){width=65%}](../studyguides/rationalizingthedenominator.qmd)

:::

## Want more? {data-background-image="slides-bg.png"}

- **get started with quarto**

     - [link to my template from the SMSN network](https://tdhc153.github.io/smsn-quarto-workshop/)
     
     - [link to the source code is here](https://github.com/tdhc153/smsn-quarto-workshop)

- **loads more examples at <https://starmast.org> :)** 

- [link to starmast github repository](https://github.com/tdhc153/starmast) for all **source code**

- contact me at **tdhc@st-andrews.ac.uk**: any ideas, feedback, comments, most welcome :)

**thank you for listening!**
